zh.lifeSummary: 當Guillaume de Sabran於大約1120年出生在法國奧其坦尼加爾薩布朗時，他的父親Raymond Ier des Baux39歲，母親Étiennette de Gévaudan-Provence23歲。他和Sinispella de Lacon serra至少有1個女兒。他去世於1200年在法國奧維-o-阿爾培斯德o姆梅武易隆享年81歲。

nameMeanings.name: de
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Indian Dilip, Anirban, Gopal, Alok, Anindya, Arunava, Asit, Asok, Dibyendu, Goutam, Indra, Indranil.

variants.name: Dey
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Indian Ashit, Indu, Naina, Partha, Soumya, Ajit, Anjan, Anjana, Anup, Arjun, Arun, Ashim.
English, Scottish, and Welsh: variant of   Day  .
German: topographic name from a field name in North Rhine-Westphalia, denoting a sizeable piece of land, from Middle Low German dē ‘thigh’ (referring to the shape of the property).

nameMeanings.name: Sabran
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Jewish Eliyahu, Hersh, Hyman, Mayer, Nadav, Zev.

German and Jewish (Ashkenazic); Hungarian (Sáfrán); Croatian and Slovenian (Šafran); Czech and Slovak (Šafran and Šafrán): from Middle High German saffrān, German Safran, Hungarian sáfrány, Slavic šafran (standard Slovenian is žafran; see below) ‘saffron’, a bright yellow spice and colorant derived from the stigmas of a crocus, hence a metonymic occupational name for a spicer or a nickname for someone with reddish-yellow hair. The word is ultimately derived from Arabic zaʿfarān. The Jewish name is mainly artificial.
Americanized form of Polish Szafran and Slovenian Žafran: from Polish szafran, Slovenian žafran ‘saffron’, words of the same ultimately Arabic origin as above.

variants.name: Shafran
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Jewish Simcha, Avi, Meyer, Shoshana, Tsilya, Uri.  Russian Anatoly, Mikhail, Natalya, Oleg, Semyon, Yuriy.
Jewish (eastern Ashkenazic): mainly artificial name, cognate with Safran , from eastern Yiddish shafran, Polish szafran ‘saffron’.

variants.name: Szafranski
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Polish Andrzej, Bogdan, Halina, Krzysztof, Miroslaw, Stanislaw, Tadeuz, Wieslaw.

Polish (Szafrański): habitational name for someone from either of two places called Szafranki in Masovian and Podlaskie voivodeships, or Szafranów, in Masovian and Podkarpackie voivodeships, all named with szafran ‘saffron’ (see Safran 1). Compare Safranski .

variants.name: Szafran
meanings: Polish, Rusyn (from Poland), and Jewish (from Poland): from Polish szafran ‘saffron’, a topographic name for someone who lived where the saffron crocus grew, a metonymic occupational name for a spicer, or possibly a nickname for someone with yellow hair. See also Safran .

variants.name: Saffran
meanings: German and Jewish (Ashkenazic): variant of   Safran  .

variants.name: Safranek
meanings: Czech (Šafránek): from a diminutive of Czech šafrán ‘saffron’ (see Safran ), applied as nickname for someone with reddish yellow hair.

nameMeanings.name: Guillaume
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of First Names © Patrick Hanks and Flavia Hodges 1990, 2003, 2006.

variants.name: William
meanings: Probably the most successful of all the Old French names of Germanic origin that were introduced to England by the Normans. It is derived from Germanic wil ‘will, desire’ + helm ‘helmet, protection’. The fact that it was borne by the Conqueror himself does not seem to have inhibited its favour with the ‘conquered’ population: in the first century after the Conquest it was the commonest male name of all, and not only among the Normans. In the later Middle Ages it was overtaken by John , but continued to run second to that name until the 20th century, when the picture became more fragmented.

en.lifeSummary: When Guillaume de Sabran was born about 1120, in Sabran, Gard, Occitanie, France, his father, Raymond Ier des Baux, was 39 and his mother, Étiennette de Gévaudan-Provence, was 23. He had at least 1 daughter with Sinispella de Lacon serra. He died in 1200, in Mévouillon, Drôme, Auvergne-Rhône-Alpes, France, at the age of 81.

ko.lifeSummary: Guillaume de Sabran(이)가 대략 1120년 프랑스 옥시탠이 가드 사란에서 태어났을 때, 그의 아버지 Raymond Ier des Baux(은)는 39세였고, 그의 어머니 Étiennette de Gévaudan-Provence(은)는 23세였다. 그는 Sinispella de Lacon serra(와)과 적어도 한 명의 딸을 두었다. 그는 1200년 프랑스 오버그니-ho니-알프스 드o e보우이런에서 81세의 나이로 사망했다.

ru.lifeSummary: Когда Guillaume de Sabran родился около 1120 г. в местности сабран, гард, окситани, Франция, его отцу по имени Raymond Ier des Baux было 39 лет, а матери, которую звали Étiennette de Gévaudan-Provence, 23. У него была по крайней мере 1 дочь от Sinispella de Lacon serra. Он умер в 1200 г. в местности мeвоуиллон, дрoм, аувиргне-рoне-алпесь, Франция в возрасте 81 год.
